docs.title: ITS Ontology for Location Referencing
docs.text:
Ontology that imports pattern modules (core, point, linear, area, and itinerary) that define the concepts for location referencing.

The ITS Ontology for Location Referencing consists of the following patterns:

  • Area
  • Core
  • Itinerary
  • Linear
  • Point

The formal definition of this ontology is available in TURTLE Syntax.

docs.title: AreaByCircle
docs.text: An area geometry encoded as a circle.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated AreaGeometry AreaGeometry AreaByCircle AreaByCircle AreaByCircle->AreaGeometry cdm1_Length cdm1:Length AreaByCircle->cdm1_Length radius PointFeature PointFeature AreaByCircle->PointFeature hasCenter

docs.title: Formalization for AreaByCircle
docs.text: Property Constraint hasCenter only PointFeature radius only cdm1:Length subClassOf AreaGeometry

docs.title: AreaByCode
docs.text: An area geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated AreaGeometry AreaGeometry CodedGeometry CodedGeometry AreaByCode AreaByCode AreaByCode->AreaGeometry AreaByCode->CodedGeometry

docs.title: Formalization for AreaByCode
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf AreaGeometry subClassOf CodedGeometry

docs.title: AreaByGrid
docs.text: An area geometry encoded as a grid. The rectangle defined by lower-left and upper-right is the base cell, which is replicated eastward (columns) and northward (rows).

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated AreaByRectangle AreaByRectangle AreaGeometry AreaGeometry AreaByGrid AreaByGrid numColumns : xsd:nonNegativeInteger numRows : xsd:nonNegativeInteger AreaByGrid->AreaByRectangle AreaByGrid->AreaGeometry

docs.title: Formalization for AreaByGrid
docs.text: Property Constraint numColumns datatype xsd:nonNegativeInteger numRows datatype xsd:nonNegativeInteger subClassOf AreaGeometry subClassOf AreaByRectangle

docs.title: AreaByLinearBoundaries
docs.text: An area geometry encoded as a set of linear boundary geometries.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated AreaGeometry AreaGeometry AreaByLinearBoundaries AreaByLinearBoundaries AreaByLinearBoundaries->AreaGeometry LinearFeature LinearFeature AreaByLinearBoundaries->LinearFeature hasLinearBoundary 1..*

docs.title: Formalization for AreaByLinearBoundaries
docs.text: Property Constraint hasLinearBoundary min 1 LinearFeature subClassOf AreaGeometry

docs.title: AreaByMultiPolygon
docs.text: An area geometry encoded as a MultiPolygon geometry.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated AreaGeometry AreaGeometry sf_MultiPolygon sf:MultiPolygon AreaByMultiPolygon AreaByMultiPolygon AreaByMultiPolygon->AreaGeometry AreaByMultiPolygon->sf_MultiPolygon

docs.title: Formalization for AreaByMultiPolygon
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf AreaGeometry subClassOf sf:MultiPolygon

docs.title: AreaByPolygon
docs.text: An area geometry encoded as a Polygon geometry.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated AreaGeometry AreaGeometry sf_Polygon sf:Polygon AreaByPolygon AreaByPolygon AreaByPolygon->AreaGeometry AreaByPolygon->sf_Polygon

docs.title: Formalization for AreaByPolygon
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf AreaGeometry subClassOf sf:Polygon

docs.title: AreaByRectangle
docs.text: An area geometry encoded as a rectangle, defined by a lower-left corner and an upper-right corner.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated AreaGeometry AreaGeometry AreaByRectangle AreaByRectangle AreaByRectangle->AreaGeometry PointFeature PointFeature AreaByRectangle->PointFeature hasLowerLeft AreaByRectangle->PointFeature hasUpperRight

docs.title: Specializations of AreaByRectangle
docs.text: Class Description Area By Grid An area geometry encoded as a grid. The rectangle defined by lower-left and upper-right is the base cell, which is replicated eastward (columns) and northward (rows).

docs.title: Formalization for AreaByRectangle
docs.text: Property Constraint hasLowerLeft only PointFeature hasUpperRight only PointFeature subClassOf AreaGeometry

docs.title: AreaFeature
docs.text: A spatial feature enclosed within a two-dimensional boundary or boundaries across a defined surface.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated Feature Feature AreaFeature AreaFeature AreaFeature->Feature

docs.title: Formalization for AreaFeature
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf Feature

docs.title: AreaGeometry
docs.text:
A geometry that encodes an area location using a specific method.

NOTE: The boundary can consist of a single curvilinear line (e.g. a circle) or multiple curvilinear lines (e.g. the boundaries of city limits) and can consist of multiple enclosed areas.

EXAMPLE: The British Isles.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated Geometry Geometry AreaGeometry AreaGeometry AreaGeometry->Geometry

docs.title: Specializations of AreaGeometry
docs.text: Class Description Area By Circle An area geometry encoded as a circle. Area By Code An area geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Area By Grid An area geometry encoded as a grid. The rectangle defined by lower-left and upper-right is the base cell, which is replicated eastward (columns) and northward (rows). Area By Grid An area geometry encoded as a grid. The rectangle defined by lower-left and upper-right is the base cell, which is replicated eastward (columns) and northward (rows). Area By Linear Boundaries An area geometry encoded as a set of linear boundary geometries. Area By Multi Polygon An area geometry encoded as a MultiPolygon geometry. Area By Polygon An area geometry encoded as a Polygon geometry. Area By Rectangle An area geometry encoded as a rectangle, defined by a lower-left corner and an upper-right corner.

docs.title: Formalization for AreaGeometry
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf Geometry

docs.title: ITS Area Referencing
docs.text:
Methodology module for area-based locations and area location geometries.

This pattern imports the following files:

  • https://w3id.org/itsdata/location/v1/LinearPattern
  • https://w3id.org/itsdata/location/v1/LocationCorePattern

This pattern consists of the following classes:

  • Area By Circle
  • Area By Code
  • Area By Grid
  • Area By Linear Boundaries
  • Area By Multi Polygon
  • Area By Polygon
  • Area By Rectangle
  • Area Feature

  • Area Geometry This module defines the following properties:

  • hasCenter

  • hasLinearBoundary
  • hasLowerLeft
  • hasUpperRight
  • numColumns
  • numRows
  • radius

The formal definition of this pattern is available in TURTLE Syntax in two files, the core semantics and the SHACL restrictions.

docs.title: CodedGeometry
docs.text: A geometry whose extent is resolved via an external code, registry, or service (a pointer to a geometry held elsewhere).

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated Geometry Geometry CodedGeometry CodedGeometry CodedGeometry->Geometry LocationCode LocationCode CodedGeometry->LocationCode hasLookupCode

docs.title: Specializations of CodedGeometry
docs.text: Class Description Area By Code An area geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Itinerary Code An itinerary geometry whose route geometry is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external itinerary/route referencing system. Linear By Code A linear geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Point By Code A point geometry whose coordinates are not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system.

docs.title: Formalization for CodedGeometry
docs.text: Property Constraint hasLookupCode only LocationCode subClassOf Geometry

docs.title: CoordinateGeometry
docs.text: A geometry that is represented by a coordinate system (i.e., directly encodes coordinate tuples).

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated Geometry Geometry CoordinateGeometry CoordinateGeometry coordinateEpoch : xsd:dateTime CoordinateGeometry->Geometry CoordinateReferenceSystem CoordinateReferenceSystem CoordinateGeometry->CoordinateReferenceSystem hasCRS

docs.title: Specializations of CoordinateGeometry
docs.text: Class Description Point By Coordinates A coordinate tuple defining the geodetic position of a single point location using a known geodetic reference system Point By Geo Coordinates A point location geometry encoded as latitude/longitude and optional elements, such as elevation and metadata. Point By Projected Coordinates A point location geometry encoded as projected coordinates and optional elements, such as elevation and metadata.

docs.title: Formalization for CoordinateGeometry
docs.text: Property Constraint coordinateEpoch datatype xsd:dateTime hasCRS only CoordinateReferenceSystem subClassOf Geometry

docs.title: CoordinateReferenceSystem
docs.text: A coordinate reference system (CRS) used to interpret coordinate tuples in a CoordinateGeometry. Typically identified using an OGC CRS IRI such as http://www.opengis.net/def/crs/EPSG/0/4326.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated LocationThing LocationThing CoordinateReferenceSystem CoordinateReferenceSystem CoordinateReferenceSystem->LocationThing

docs.title: Formalization for CoordinateReferenceSystem
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf LocationThing

docs.title: DistanceAccuracy
docs.text: A statement of distance accuracy for a point geometry.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated LocationThing LocationThing DistanceAccuracy DistanceAccuracy DistanceAccuracy->LocationThing cdm1_Length cdm1:Length DistanceAccuracy->cdm1_Length hasDistanceAccuracy cdm1_Ratio cdm1:Ratio DistanceAccuracy->cdm1_Ratio confidencePercentage MeasurementErrorCode MeasurementErrorCode DistanceAccuracy->MeasurementErrorCode distanceAccuracyErrorCode

docs.title: Specializations of DistanceAccuracy
docs.text: Class Description Elevation Accuracy A statement of elevation accuracy for a point geometry. Position Accuracy A statement of positional accuracy for a point geometry.

docs.title: Formalization for DistanceAccuracy
docs.text: Property Constraint confidencePercentage only cdm1:Ratio distanceAccuracyErrorCode only MeasurementErrorCode hasDistanceAccuracy only cdm1:Length subClassOf LocationThing

docs.title: Elevation
docs.text: An elevation for a point location along with metadata.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated LocationThing LocationThing Elevation Elevation Elevation->LocationThing cdm1_Length cdm1:Length Elevation->cdm1_Length hasElevationDistance ElevationAccuracy ElevationAccuracy Elevation->ElevationAccuracy hasElevationAccuracy ElevationHeightCode ElevationHeightCode Elevation->ElevationHeightCode hasElevationHeightCode ElevationReferenceCode ElevationReferenceCode Elevation->ElevationReferenceCode hasElevationReference

docs.title: Formalization for Elevation
docs.text: Property Constraint hasElevationAccuracy only ElevationAccuracy hasElevationDistance only cdm1:Length hasElevationHeightCode only ElevationHeightCode hasElevationReference only ElevationReferenceCode subClassOf LocationThing

docs.title: ElevationAccuracy
docs.text: A statement of elevation accuracy for a point geometry.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated DistanceAccuracy DistanceAccuracy ElevationAccuracy ElevationAccuracy ElevationAccuracy->DistanceAccuracy

docs.title: Formalization for ElevationAccuracy
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf DistanceAccuracy

docs.title: ElevationHeightCode
docs.text: A code that identifies the height value for an elevation value (e.g., 'ground layer 1', 'elevated layer 1', 'elevated layer 2', 'tunnel layer 1').

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated its-core_Code its-core:Code LocationThing LocationThing ElevationHeightCode ElevationHeightCode ElevationHeightCode->its-core_Code ElevationHeightCode->LocationThing

docs.title: Formalization for ElevationHeightCode
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf its-core:Code subClassOf LocationThing

docs.title: ElevationReferenceCode
docs.text: A code that identifies the reference system for an elevation value (e.g., 'ellipsoidal', 'gravityRelated', 'ground level', 'EPSG:5701', 'coded height').

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated its-core_Code its-core:Code LocationThing LocationThing ElevationReferenceCode ElevationReferenceCode ElevationReferenceCode->its-core_Code ElevationReferenceCode->LocationThing

docs.title: Formalization for ElevationReferenceCode
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf its-core:Code subClassOf LocationThing

docs.title: Feature
docs.text:
An abstraction of real-world phenomena. An ITS-domain feature (subclass of geo:Feature) used to model real-world things that have a spatial location.

NOTE: Geographic features can be defined for any geographic entity that can be defined with geospatial boundaries, such as landmarks, buildings, transport infrastructure (e.g., road, pathway, railway, bridge structures, intersections, traffic signal cabinet, traffic signal pole, waterway), jurisdictional areas (e.g., country, city), etc.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated geo_Feature geo:Feature SpatialObject SpatialObject Feature Feature cdm1:hasName : rdf:langString Feature->geo_Feature Feature->SpatialObject

docs.title: Specializations of Feature
docs.text: Class Description Area Feature A spatial feature enclosed within a two-dimensional boundary or boundaries across a defined surface. Itinerary An ordered set of multiple physically separate features forming a route or itinerary. Linear Feature A spatial feature that extends along a defined path (typically between two point features or along a network element). Location Group An unordered set of multiple physically separate features (each typically a singular feature with its own geometry). Point Feature A spatial feature with no length in any of the spatial dimensions (a point phenomenon in space).

docs.title: Formalization for Feature
docs.text: Property Constraint cdm1:hasName datatype rdf:langString subClassOf SpatialObject subClassOf geo:Feature

docs.title: FeatureList
docs.text: An ordered list of member features (e.g., itinerary waypoints).

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated rdf_List rdf:List FeatureList FeatureList rdf:first FeatureList->rdf_List

docs.title: Formalization for FeatureList
docs.text: Property Constraint rdf:first only Feature subClassOf rdf:List

docs.title: Geometry
docs.text:
A description of a spatial location in the real world according to a defined reference system.

NOTE: A Geometry can be complex, such as a jurisdictional area that consists of multiple islands.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated geo_Geometry geo:Geometry SpatialObject SpatialObject Geometry Geometry Geometry->geo_Geometry Geometry->SpatialObject

docs.title: Specializations of Geometry
docs.text: Class Description Area By Circle An area geometry encoded as a circle. Area By Code An area geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Area By Code An area geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Area By Grid An area geometry encoded as a grid. The rectangle defined by lower-left and upper-right is the base cell, which is replicated eastward (columns) and northward (rows). Area By Grid An area geometry encoded as a grid. The rectangle defined by lower-left and upper-right is the base cell, which is replicated eastward (columns) and northward (rows). Area By Linear Boundaries An area geometry encoded as a set of linear boundary geometries. Area By Multi Polygon An area geometry encoded as a MultiPolygon geometry. Area By Polygon An area geometry encoded as a Polygon geometry. Area By Rectangle An area geometry encoded as a rectangle, defined by a lower-left corner and an upper-right corner. Area Geometry A geometry that encodes an area location using a specific method. Coded Geometry A geometry whose extent is resolved via an external code, registry, or service (a pointer to a geometry held elsewhere). Coordinate Geometry A geometry that is represented by a coordinate system (i.e., directly encodes coordinate tuples). Itinerary By Waypoints An itinerary geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of features (waypoints). Itinerary Code An itinerary geometry whose route geometry is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external itinerary/route referencing system. Itinerary Code An itinerary geometry whose route geometry is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external itinerary/route referencing system. Itinerary Geometry A geometry that encodes an itinerary using a specific method. Linear By Code A linear geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Linear By Code A linear geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Linear By Linear Ring A linear geometry encoded as a LinearRing geometry. Linear By Line String A linear geometry encoded as a LineString geometry. Linear By Multi Line String A linear geometry encoded as a MultiLineString geometry. Linear By Point Features A linear geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of points. Linear By Point Geometries A linear geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of point geometries. Linear Geometry A geometry that encodes a linear location using a specific method. Point By Code A point geometry whose coordinates are not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Point By Code A point geometry whose coordinates are not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Point By Coordinates A coordinate tuple defining the geodetic position of a single point location using a known geodetic reference system Point By Coordinates A coordinate tuple defining the geodetic position of a single point location using a known geodetic reference system Point By Geo Coordinates A point location geometry encoded as latitude/longitude and optional elements, such as elevation and metadata. Point By Linear Position A point geometry defined by an offset along a linear geometry. Point By Projected Coordinates A point location geometry encoded as projected coordinates and optional elements, such as elevation and metadata. Point Geometry A geometry for a point location using a specific method (e.g., coordinates or an external code).

docs.title: Formalization for Geometry
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf SpatialObject subClassOf geo:Geometry

docs.title: Itinerary
docs.text: An ordered set of multiple physically separate features forming a route or itinerary.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated Feature Feature Itinerary Itinerary geo:hasDefaultGeometry geo:hasGeometry Itinerary->Feature

docs.title: Formalization for Itinerary
docs.text: Property Constraint geo:hasDefaultGeometry only ItineraryGeometry geo:hasGeometry only ItineraryGeometry subClassOf Feature

docs.title: ItineraryByWaypoints
docs.text: An itinerary geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of features (waypoints).

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated ItineraryGeometry ItineraryGeometry ItineraryByWaypoints ItineraryByWaypoints ItineraryByWaypoints->ItineraryGeometry FeatureList FeatureList ItineraryByWaypoints->FeatureList hasWaypointList

docs.title: Formalization for ItineraryByWaypoints
docs.text: Property Constraint hasWaypointList only FeatureList subClassOf ItineraryGeometry

docs.title: ItineraryCode
docs.text: An itinerary geometry whose route geometry is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external itinerary/route referencing system.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated CodedGeometry CodedGeometry ItineraryGeometry ItineraryGeometry ItineraryCode ItineraryCode ItineraryCode->CodedGeometry ItineraryCode->ItineraryGeometry

docs.title: Formalization for ItineraryCode
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf ItineraryGeometry subClassOf CodedGeometry

docs.title: ItineraryGeometry
docs.text: A geometry that encodes an itinerary using a specific method.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated Geometry Geometry ItineraryGeometry ItineraryGeometry ItineraryGeometry->Geometry

docs.title: Specializations of ItineraryGeometry
docs.text: Class Description Itinerary By Waypoints An itinerary geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of features (waypoints). Itinerary Code An itinerary geometry whose route geometry is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external itinerary/route referencing system.

docs.title: Formalization for ItineraryGeometry
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf Geometry

docs.title: ITS Itinerary Referencing
docs.text:
Methodology module for itineraries (ordered sets of features) and itinerary geometries.

This pattern imports the following files:

  • https://w3id.org/itsdata/location/v1/LocationCorePattern

This pattern consists of the following classes:

  • Feature List
  • Itinerary
  • Itinerary By Waypoints
  • Itinerary Code

  • Itinerary Geometry This module defines the following properties:

  • hasWaypointList

  • locationContainedInItinerary

The formal definition of this pattern is available in TURTLE Syntax in two files, the core semantics and the SHACL restrictions.

docs.title: LinearByCode
docs.text: A linear geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated CodedGeometry CodedGeometry LinearGeometry LinearGeometry LinearByCode LinearByCode LinearByCode->CodedGeometry LinearByCode->LinearGeometry

docs.title: Formalization for LinearByCode
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf LinearGeometry subClassOf CodedGeometry

docs.text: A linear geometry encoded as a LineString geometry.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

docs.title: Formalization for LinearByLineString
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf LinearGeometry subClassOf sf:LineString

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

docs.title: Formalization for LinearByLinearRing
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf LinearGeometry subClassOf sf:LinearRing

docs.title: LinearByMultiLineString
docs.text: A linear geometry encoded as a MultiLineString geometry.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated LinearGeometry LinearGeometry sf_MultiLineString sf:MultiLineString LinearByMultiLineString LinearByMultiLineString LinearByMultiLineString->LinearGeometry LinearByMultiLineString->sf_MultiLineString

docs.title: Formalization for LinearByMultiLineString
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf LinearGeometry subClassOf sf:MultiLineString

docs.title: LinearByPointFeatures
docs.text: A linear geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of points.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated LinearGeometry LinearGeometry LinearByPointFeatures LinearByPointFeatures LinearByPointFeatures->LinearGeometry PointFeatureList PointFeatureList LinearByPointFeatures->PointFeatureList hasPointList

docs.text: Property Constraint hasPointList only PointFeatureList subClassOf LinearGeometry

docs.title: LinearByPointGeometries
docs.text: A linear geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of point geometries.

docs.title: Diagram

SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated LinearGeometry LinearGeometry LinearByPointGeometries LinearByPointGeometries LinearByPointGeometries->LinearGeometry PointGeometryList PointGeometryList LinearByPointGeometries->PointGeometryList hasPointGeometryList

docs.title: Formalization for LinearByPointGeometries
docs.text: Property Constraint hasPointGeometryList only PointGeometryList subClassOf LinearGeometry

docs.title: LinearFeature
docs.text:
A spatial feature that extends along a defined path (typically between two point features or along a network element).

NOTE: The path can exhibit 3-dimensional characteristics.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated Feature Feature LinearFeature LinearFeature LinearFeature->Feature

docs.title: Formalization for LinearFeature
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf Feature

docs.title: LinearGeometry
docs.text: A geometry that encodes a linear location using a specific method.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated Geometry Geometry LinearGeometry LinearGeometry LinearGeometry->Geometry

docs.title: Specializations of LinearGeometry
docs.text: Class Description Linear By Code A linear geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Linear By Linear Ring A linear geometry encoded as a LinearRing geometry. Linear By Line String A linear geometry encoded as a LineString geometry. Linear By Multi Line String A linear geometry encoded as a MultiLineString geometry. Linear By Point Features A linear geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of points. Linear By Point Geometries A linear geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of point geometries.

docs.title: Formalization for LinearGeometry
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf Geometry

docs.title: ITS Linear Referencing
docs.text:
Methodology module for linear elements, linear locations, and linear location geometries.

This pattern imports the following files:

  • https://w3id.org/itsdata/location/v1/LocationCorePattern
  • https://w3id.org/itsdata/location/v1/PointPattern

This pattern consists of the following classes:

  • Linear By Code
  • Linear By Linear Ring
  • Linear By Line String
  • Linear By Multi Line String
  • Linear By Point Features
  • Linear By Point Geometries
  • Linear Feature
  • Linear Geometry
  • Offset Distance
  • Point By Linear Position
  • Point Feature List

  • Point Geometry List This module defines the following properties:

  • alongLinearGeometry

  • basePoint
  • directionPoint
  • hasOffsetDistance
  • hasPointGeometryList
  • hasPointList
  • offsetLength
  • offsetPercentage

The formal definition of this pattern is available in TURTLE Syntax in two files, the core semantics and the SHACL restrictions.

docs.title: LocationCode
docs.text: A code that identifies (or can be used to look up) a location in some external referencing system.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated its-core_Code its-core:Code LocationThing LocationThing LocationCode LocationCode LocationCode->its-core_Code LocationCode->LocationThing

docs.title: Formalization for LocationCode
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf LocationThing subClassOf its-core:Code

docs.title: ITS Location Referencing Core
docs.text:
Core abstractions for spatial concepts, including features, geometries, and feature collections used across multiple location-referencing methodologies.

This pattern imports the following files:

  • https://w3id.org/itsdata/core/v1/

This pattern consists of the following classes:

  • Coded Geometry
  • Coordinate Geometry
  • Coordinate Reference System
  • Feature
  • Geometry
  • Location Code
  • Location Group
  • Location Thing

  • Spatial Object This module defines the following properties:

  • coordinateEpoch

  • geometryOf
  • hasCRS
  • hasLookupCode
  • hasMember
  • LocationDataProperty
  • LocationObjectProperty

The formal definition of this pattern is available in TURTLE Syntax in two files, the core semantics and the SHACL restrictions.

docs.title: LocationGroup
docs.text: An unordered set of multiple physically separate features (each typically a singular feature with its own geometry).

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated Feature Feature LocationGroup LocationGroup LocationGroup->Feature LocationGroup->Feature hasMember 2..*

docs.title: Formalization for LocationGroup
docs.text: Property Constraint hasMember min 2 Feature subClassOf Feature

docs.title: LocationThing
docs.text: Parent class for location domain concepts.

docs.title: Diagram
docs.text:
SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated LocationThing LocationThing

docs.title: Specializations of LocationThing
docs.text: Class Description Area By Circle An area geometry encoded as a circle. Area By Code An area geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Area By Code An area geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Area By Grid An area geometry encoded as a grid. The rectangle defined by lower-left and upper-right is the base cell, which is replicated eastward (columns) and northward (rows). Area By Grid An area geometry encoded as a grid. The rectangle defined by lower-left and upper-right is the base cell, which is replicated eastward (columns) and northward (rows). Area By Linear Boundaries An area geometry encoded as a set of linear boundary geometries. Area By Multi Polygon An area geometry encoded as a MultiPolygon geometry. Area By Polygon An area geometry encoded as a Polygon geometry. Area By Rectangle An area geometry encoded as a rectangle, defined by a lower-left corner and an upper-right corner. Area Feature A spatial feature enclosed within a two-dimensional boundary or boundaries across a defined surface. Area Geometry A geometry that encodes an area location using a specific method. Coded Geometry A geometry whose extent is resolved via an external code, registry, or service (a pointer to a geometry held elsewhere). Coordinate Geometry A geometry that is represented by a coordinate system (i.e., directly encodes coordinate tuples). Coordinate Reference System A coordinate reference system (CRS) used to interpret coordinate tuples in a CoordinateGeometry. Typically identified using an OGC CRS IRI such as http://www.opengis.net/def/crs/EPSG/0/4326. Distance Accuracy A statement of distance accuracy for a point geometry. Elevation An elevation for a point location along with metadata. Elevation Accuracy A statement of elevation accuracy for a point geometry. Elevation Height Code A code that identifies the height value for an elevation value  (e.g., 'ground layer 1', 'elevated layer 1', 'elevated layer 2', 'tunnel layer 1'). Elevation Reference Code A code that identifies the reference system for an elevation value  (e.g., 'ellipsoidal', 'gravityRelated', 'ground level', 'EPSG:5701', 'coded height'). Feature An abstraction of real-world phenomena. An ITS-domain feature (subclass of geo:Feature) used to model real-world things that have a spatial location. Geometry A description of a spatial location in the real world according to a defined reference system. Itinerary An ordered set of multiple physically separate features forming a route or itinerary. Itinerary By Waypoints An itinerary geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of features (waypoints). Itinerary Code An itinerary geometry whose route geometry is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external itinerary/route referencing system. Itinerary Code An itinerary geometry whose route geometry is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external itinerary/route referencing system. Itinerary Geometry A geometry that encodes an itinerary using a specific method. Linear By Code A linear geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Linear By Code A linear geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Linear By Linear Ring A linear geometry encoded as a LinearRing geometry. Linear By Line String A linear geometry encoded as a LineString geometry. Linear By Multi Line String A linear geometry encoded as a MultiLineString geometry. Linear By Point Features A linear geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of points. Linear By Point Geometries A linear geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of point geometries. Linear Feature A spatial feature that extends along a defined path (typically between two point features or along a network element). Linear Geometry A geometry that encodes a linear location using a specific method. Location Code A code that identifies (or can be used to look up) a location in some external referencing system. Location Group An unordered set of multiple physically separate features (each typically a singular feature with its own geometry). Measurement Error Code A code identifying an error condition or qualification for a measurement value. Offset Distance An offset distance expressed either as a length or as a percentage. Point By Code A point geometry whose coordinates are not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Point By Code A point geometry whose coordinates are not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Point By Coordinates A coordinate tuple defining the geodetic position of a single point location using a known geodetic reference system Point By Coordinates A coordinate tuple defining the geodetic position of a single point location using a known geodetic reference system Point By Geo Coordinates A point location geometry encoded as latitude/longitude and optional elements, such as elevation and metadata. Point By Linear Position A point geometry defined by an offset along a linear geometry. Point By Projected Coordinates A point location geometry encoded as projected coordinates and optional elements, such as elevation and metadata. Point Feature A spatial feature with no length in any of the spatial dimensions (a point phenomenon in space). Point Geometry A geometry for a point location using a specific method (e.g., coordinates or an external code). Position Accuracy A statement of positional accuracy for a point geometry. Position Confidence Ellipse A confidence ellipse describing uncertainty of a point position in a horizontal plane. Spatial Object A spatial object within the ITS domain (subclass of geo:SpatialObject).

docs.title: ITS Point Referencing
docs.text:
A module of the ITS Location Ontology that defines concepts for point-based locations and geometries of those locations.

This pattern imports the following files:

  • https://w3id.org/itsdata/location/v1/LocationCorePattern

This pattern consists of the following classes:

  • Distance Accuracy
  • Elevation
  • Elevation Accuracy
  • Elevation Height Code
  • Elevation Reference Code
  • Measurement Error Code
  • Point By Code
  • Point By Coordinates
  • Point By Geo Coordinates
  • Point By Projected Coordinates
  • Point Feature
  • Point Geometry
  • Position Accuracy

  • Position Confidence Ellipse This module defines the following properties:

  • bearing

  • confidencePercentage
  • distanceAccuracyErrorCode
  • easting
  • hasDistanceAccuracy
  • hasElevation
  • hasElevationAccuracy
  • hasElevationDistance
  • hasElevationHeightCode
  • hasElevationReference
  • hasPositionAccuracy
  • hasPositionConfidenceEllipse
  • latitude
  • longitude
  • northing
  • semiMajorAxisLength
  • semiMajorAxisLengthErrorCode
  • semiMajorAxisOrientation
  • semiMajorAxisOrientationErrorCode
  • semiMinorAxisLength
  • semiMinorAxisLengthErrorCode

The formal definition of this pattern is available in TURTLE Syntax in two files, the core semantics and the SHACL restrictions.

docs.title: PositionConfidenceEllipse
docs.text: A confidence ellipse describing uncertainty of a point position in a horizontal plane.

docs.title: Diagram

SVG (interactive)PNG

cluster_associated LocationThing LocationThing PositionConfidenceEllipse PositionConfidenceEllipse PositionConfidenceEllipse->LocationThing cdm1_Length cdm1:Length PositionConfidenceEllipse->cdm1_Length semiMajorAxisLength PositionConfidenceEllipse->cdm1_Length semiMinorAxisLength its-core_Bearing its-core:Bearing PositionConfidenceEllipse->its-core_Bearing semiMajorAxisOrientation MeasurementErrorCode MeasurementErrorCode PositionConfidenceEllipse->MeasurementErrorCode semiMajorAxisLengthErrorCode PositionConfidenceEllipse->MeasurementErrorCode semiMinorAxisLengthErrorCode PositionConfidenceEllipse->MeasurementErrorCode semiMajorAxisOrientationErrorCode

docs.title: Formalization for PositionConfidenceEllipse
docs.text: Property Constraint semiMajorAxisLength only cdm1:Length semiMajorAxisLengthErrorCode only MeasurementErrorCode semiMajorAxisOrientation only its-core:Bearing semiMajorAxisOrientationErrorCode only MeasurementErrorCode semiMinorAxisLength only cdm1:Length semiMinorAxisLengthErrorCode only MeasurementErrorCode subClassOf LocationThing

docs.title: Specializations of SpatialObject
docs.text: Class Description Area By Circle An area geometry encoded as a circle. Area By Code An area geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Area By Code An area geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Area By Grid An area geometry encoded as a grid. The rectangle defined by lower-left and upper-right is the base cell, which is replicated eastward (columns) and northward (rows). Area By Grid An area geometry encoded as a grid. The rectangle defined by lower-left and upper-right is the base cell, which is replicated eastward (columns) and northward (rows). Area By Linear Boundaries An area geometry encoded as a set of linear boundary geometries. Area By Multi Polygon An area geometry encoded as a MultiPolygon geometry. Area By Polygon An area geometry encoded as a Polygon geometry. Area By Rectangle An area geometry encoded as a rectangle, defined by a lower-left corner and an upper-right corner. Area Feature A spatial feature enclosed within a two-dimensional boundary or boundaries across a defined surface. Area Geometry A geometry that encodes an area location using a specific method. Coded Geometry A geometry whose extent is resolved via an external code, registry, or service (a pointer to a geometry held elsewhere). Coordinate Geometry A geometry that is represented by a coordinate system (i.e., directly encodes coordinate tuples). Feature An abstraction of real-world phenomena. An ITS-domain feature (subclass of geo:Feature) used to model real-world things that have a spatial location. Geometry A description of a spatial location in the real world according to a defined reference system. Itinerary An ordered set of multiple physically separate features forming a route or itinerary. Itinerary By Waypoints An itinerary geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of features (waypoints). Itinerary Code An itinerary geometry whose route geometry is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external itinerary/route referencing system. Itinerary Code An itinerary geometry whose route geometry is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external itinerary/route referencing system. Itinerary Geometry A geometry that encodes an itinerary using a specific method. Linear By Code A linear geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Linear By Code A linear geometry whose extent is not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Linear By Linear Ring A linear geometry encoded as a LinearRing geometry. Linear By Line String A linear geometry encoded as a LineString geometry. Linear By Multi Line String A linear geometry encoded as a MultiLineString geometry. Linear By Point Features A linear geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of points. Linear By Point Geometries A linear geometry encoded as an ordered sequence of point geometries. Linear Feature A spatial feature that extends along a defined path (typically between two point features or along a network element). Linear Geometry A geometry that encodes a linear location using a specific method. Location Group An unordered set of multiple physically separate features (each typically a singular feature with its own geometry). Point By Code A point geometry whose coordinates are not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Point By Code A point geometry whose coordinates are not modelled here but can be resolved using :hasLookupCode and an external location referencing system. Point By Coordinates A coordinate tuple defining the geodetic position of a single point location using a known geodetic reference system Point By Coordinates A coordinate tuple defining the geodetic position of a single point location using a known geodetic reference system Point By Geo Coordinates A point location geometry encoded as latitude/longitude and optional elements, such as elevation and metadata. Point By Linear Position A point geometry defined by an offset along a linear geometry. Point By Projected Coordinates A point location geometry encoded as projected coordinates and optional elements, such as elevation and metadata. Point Feature A spatial feature with no length in any of the spatial dimensions (a point phenomenon in space). Point Geometry A geometry for a point location using a specific method (e.g., coordinates or an external code).

docs.title: Formalization for SpatialObject
docs.text: Property Constraint subClassOf LocationThing subClassOf geo:SpatialObject

docs.title: Standard adapters (scaffold)
docs.text:
This directory is reserved for standard-specific adapter modules that align external standards (e.g., Alert-C, OpenLR, TPEG) to the methodology modules in ../.

Adapters should prefer: - subclassing / equivalence axioms - annotation provenance and mapping notes - controlled enumerations where required Adapters should avoid duplicating shared primitives (coordinates, geometry, quality, external IDs).
